_comment: REFERENCE ONLY — this file documents the shape of the GENERATED public manifest (web/public/tilesets/limezu-modern-office/manifest.json). It is not read by scripts/sync_limezu_assets.mjs and does not need to be copied anywhere. To produce the real thing: place your purchased LimeZu 'Modern Office Revamped' and 'Modern Interiors' packages under assets/tilesets/limezu-modern-office/ (see ../../README.md 'LimeZu 購買與放置說明' for the exact expected sub-paths), then run `node scripts/sync_limezu_assets.mjs`. The script scans both packages, classifies every 32x32 single-item PNG into a category, copies the referenced files into web/public/tilesets/limezu-modern-office/, and writes manifest.json there — no manual per-machine manifest editing is needed (ADR-003 D1).
_schema_comment: `sprites` — object keyed by one of the 10 LayoutKind values consumed by web/src/panels/LayoutEditorPanel.tsx (see its `KINDS` array): desk, exec_desk, chair, partition, meeting_table, cabinet, printer, plant, pantry_counter, whiteboard. Each entry `{file, image, fit}` is a hand-picked representative Office single (see SPRITE_PICKS in the sync script) so furniture placed by kind alone still renders a real image instead of a color block. `catalog` — array of every scanned single-item PNG (5800+ entries): `{id, label, file, image, kind, category, categoryLabel, fit}`. `id` is globally unique — "office-<N>" (unpadded) for the Office source, "<category>-<NNN>" (3-digit zero-padded) for each of the 24 Interiors theme-sorter categories. `kind` is one of the same 10 LayoutKind values (inferred per-item for Office via number ranges; a fixed per-category default for theme items, since there's no reliable per-item heuristic across 24 unrelated vendor numbering schemes). `category`/`categoryLabel` are the machine slug (e.g. "kitchen") and 繁中 label (e.g. "廚房") used for the frontend's category browser. `categories` — array of `{slug, label, count}`, one per category actually found in the scanned package ("office" plus however many of the 24 Interiors theme folders exist on disk — ADR-003 documented 26 but only 24 exist in the purchased package; folders "1_" and "17_" are absent, see the sync script's THEME_CATEGORIES comment). Any other top-level keys (`generatedFrom`, `generatedAt`) are informational only.
